title: Express Protocol NEBNextÂź ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Library Prep Kit (IlluminaÂź) (NEB #E7650S/L) without PCR Bead Cleanup
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eejth","text":"This protocol details methods for the NEBNextÂź ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Library Prep Kit (IlluminaÂź), NEB #E7650S/L 24/96 reactions, express protocol. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8f49l","text":"\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"648g9","text":"This protocol does not include a cleanup step for each sample after cDNA synthesis and ligation. Performing the cleanup step creates libraries with higher yields and less likelihood for adaptor dimer. Skipping these steps reduces hands on time, but may require an extra cleanup at the end of library prep of the pooled sample and we have added an extra PCR cycle. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":14,"length":81}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"62cda","text":"Overview ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5crnh","text":"The NEBNext SARS-CoV-2 Library Prep Kit (Illumina) contains the enzymes, buffers and oligos required to convert a broad range of total RNA input amounts into targeted, high quality libraries for next-generation sequencing on the Illumina platform. Primers targeting the human EDF1 (NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix 1) and NEDD8 (NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix 2) genes are supplied as optional internal controls. The fast, user-friendly workflow also has minimal hands-on time. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6o4nq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8sf0q","text":"Each kit component must pass rigorous quality control standards, and for each new lot the entire set of reagents is functionally validated together by construction and sequencing of an indexed library on the Illumina sequencing platform. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d0uiq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"53um","text":"For larger volume requirements, customized and bulk packaging is available by purchasing through the OEM/Bulks department at NEB. Please contact OEM@neb.com for further information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"69l69","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"agwmf","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"50u7c","text":"NEBNext ARTIC Human Primers ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5023c","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"a87d1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e2i68","text":"NEBNext ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Primer Mix 1 and 2","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":43}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4ujrg","text":"NEBNext ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Mix 1 and 2 for SARS-CoV-2 genome amplification are based on hCoV-2019/nCoV-2019 Version 3 (v3) sequences with balanced primer concentrations. before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"71hbh","text":"Note: We recommend using the express protocol for inputs of â„ 100 copies of the (SARS-CoV-2) viral genome. The use of lower input amounts may result in significant levels of adaptor dimer. In addition, we recommend setting up a no template control reaction. It is advisable to set up your reactions in the hood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":311},{"style":"italic","offset":29,"length":17},{"style":"italic","offset":298,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"erin6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8s1ib","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bff55","text":"The presence of carry-over products can interfere with sequencing accuracy, particularly for low copy targets. Therefore, it is important to carry out the appropriate no template control (NTC) reactions to demonstrate that positive reactions are meaningful.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Gently mix and spin down the LunaScript RT SuperMix reagent. Prepare the cDNA synthesis reaction as described below: %s %s For no template controls, mix the following components: %s %s

Incubate reactions in a thermocycler* with the following steps: A B C D 1 CYCLE STEP TEMP TIME CYCLES 2 Primer Annealing 25Â°C 2 minutes 1 3 cDNA Synthesis 55Â°C 20 minutes 4 Heat Inactivation 95Â°C 1 minute 5 Hold 4Â°C â *Set heated lid to 105Â°C Samples can be stored at -20 Â°C for up to a week.

4.5 Â”l cDNA input is recommended. If using less than 4.5 Â”l of cDNA, add nuclease-free water to a final volume of 4.5 Â”l. We recommend setting up the cDNA synthesis and cDNA amplification reactions in different rooms to minimize cross-contamination of future reactions. Use of the NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix 1 and 2 are optional. If used, the appropriate NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix and NEBNext ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Primer Mix should be combined prior to use. More specifically, NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix 1 should be combined with NEBNext ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Primer Mix 1 and NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix 2 with NEBNext ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Primer Mix 2. Mixing directions are listed below.

Gently mix and spin down reagents. Prepare the split pool cDNA amplification reactions as described below: For Pool Set A: If using the NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix and a 24 reaction kit , combine 0.7 Â”L, NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix 1 with 42 Â”L, NEBNext ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Primer Mix 1 in a new tube, vortex and spin down reagents. If using a 96 reaction kit , combine 2.8 Â”L, NEBNext ARTIC Primer Mix 1 with 168 Â”L, NEBNext ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Primer Mix 1 in a new tube, vortex and spin down reagents. Use 1.75 Â”L, of the combined mix for each Pool Set A reaction. %s %s For Pool Set B: If using the NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix and a 24 reaction kit , combine 0.7 Â”L, NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix 2 with 42 Â”L, NEBNext ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Primer Mix 2 in a new tube, vortex and spin down reagents. If using a 96 reaction kit , combine 2.8 Â”L, NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix 2 with 168 Â”L, NEBNext ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Primer Mix 2 in a new tube, vortex and spin down reagents. Use 1.75 Â”L, of the combined mix for each Pool Set B reaction. A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 cDNA (Step 2) 4.5 Â”l 3 (lilac) Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix 6.25 Â”l 4 NEBNext ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Primer Mix 2* 1.75 Â”l 5 Total Volume 12.5 Â”l * If using NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix 2, add 1.75 Â”l of the combined NEBNext ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Primer Mix 2 and NEBNext ARTIC Human Primer Mix 2.

Incubate reactions in a thermocycler* with the following steps: A B C D 1 CYCLE STEP TEMP TIME CYCLES 2 Initial Denaturation 98Â°C 30 seconds 1 3 Denature 95Â°C 15 seconds 35 4 Annealing/Extension 63Â°C 5 minutes 5 Hold 4Â°C â 1 *Set heated lid to 105Â°C
Combine the Pool A and Pool B PCR reactions for each sample. Samples can be stored at -20 Â°C for up to a week.
Note: When cleanup of the pooled cDNA amplicons is skipped, the amplicons must be diluted prior to library prep. Transfer 2.5 Â”l of the pooled cDNA amplicons to a fresh tube.

Add 22.5 Â”l of low TE for a final volume of 25 Â”l. To confirm the size of the 400 bp amplicons, pooled cDNA amplicons can be run on a TapeStationÂź without cleanup. To run on a TapeStation, dilute an aliquot of the pooled amplicons 10-fold with 0.1X TE Buffer and run 2 ÎŒl on a DNA High Sensitivity ScreenTape. (See Figure 8. below for example of amplicon size profile on a TapeStation). Figure 8: Example of cDNA amplicons generated from 1000 genome copies of SARS CoV-2 .
Add the following components to a sterile nuclease-free tube: %s %s

Set a 100 Â”l or 200 Â”l pipette to 25 Â”l and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube. It is important to mix well. The presence of a small amount of bubbles will not interfere with performance

Place in a thermocycler* and run the following program: %s %s If necessary, samples can be stored at -20 Â°C ; however, a slight loss in yield (~20%) may be observed. We recommend continuing with adaptor ligation before stopping.
Add the following components directly to the End Prep Reaction Mixture: %s %s Do not premix adaptor with the Ligation Master Mix.

Set a 100 Â”l or 200 Â”l pipette to 40 Â”l and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube. Caution: The NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix is very viscous. Care should be taken to ensure adequate mixing of the ligation reaction, as incomplete mixing will result in reduced ligation efficiency. The presence of a small amount of bubbles will not interfere with performance.
Incubate at 20 Â°C for 00:15:00 in a thermocycler with the heated lid off.

Add 1.5 Â”L, (red or blue) USERÂź Enzyme to the ligation mixture from the previous step. Steps 15 and 16 are only required for use with non- indexed NEBNext Adaptors. USER enzyme can be found in the NEBNext Multiplex Oligos ( www.neb.com/oligos ).

Mix well and incubate at 37 Â°C for 00:15:00 with the heated lid set to â„ 47 Â°C . Samples can be stored overnight at -20 Â°C . Only a portion of the ligation reaction (7.5 Â”l) will move forward to PCR enrichment.

Follow step-case A. if you are using the following oligos: Use option A for any NEBNext Oligo kit where index primers are supplied in tubes. These kits have the forward and reverse primers supplied in separate tubes. Primers are supplied at 10 Â”M (each). Follow step-case B. if you are using the following oligos : Use Option B for any NEBNext Oligo kit where index primers are supplied in a 96-well plate format. These kits have the forward and reverse (i7 and i5) primers combined. Primers are supplied at 10 Â”M (combined).

Add the following components to a sterile strip tube: A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME 2 Adaptor Ligated DNA Fragments (Step 16) 7.5 ÎŒl 3 (blue) NEBNext Library PCR Master Mix 12.5 ÎŒl 4 Universal PCR Primer/i5 Primer *,** 2.5 ÎŒl 5 Index (X) Primer/i7 Primer *,** 2.5 ÎŒl 6 Total Volume 25 ÎŒl * NEBNext Oligos must be purchased separately from the library prep kit. Refer to the corresponding NEBNext Oligo kit manual for determining valid barcode combinations. ** Use only one i7/primer/index primer per sample. Use only one i5 primer (or the universal primer for single index kits) per sample.
Set a 100 ÎŒl pipette to 20 ÎŒl and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube.
Place the tube on a thermocycler8 and perform PCR amplification using the following PCR cycling conditions: %s %s

The amount of NEBNext Sample Purification Beads provided here are for use with the sample contained in the exact buffer at this step. These volumes may not work properly for a cleanup at a different step in the workflow. For cleanups of samples contained in different buffer conditions, the volumes may need to be experimentally determined.
Vortex NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend.

Add 17.5 Â”L, (0.7X) resuspended beads to the PCR reaction. Mix well by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. Be careful to expel all of the liquid out of the tip during the last mix. Vortexing for 3-5 seconds on high can also be used. If centrifuging samples after mixing, be sure to stop the centrifugation before the beads start to settle out.
Incubate samples on bench top for at least 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

Place the tube/plate on an appropriate magnetic stand to separate the beads from the supernatant. If necessary, quickly spin the sample to collect the liquid from the sides of the tube or plate wells before placing on the magnetic stand.
After 5 minutes (or when the solution is clear), carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. Caution: do not discard the beads.

Add 200 Â”L, 80% freshly prepared ethanol to the tube/plate while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets.

Repeat the previous step once for a total of two washes: Add 200 Â”L, 80% freshly prepared ethanol to the tube/plate while in the magnetic stand. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. Be sure to remove all visible liquid after the second wash. If necessary, briefly spin the tube/plate, place back on the magnet and remove traces of ethanol with a p10 pipette tip.

Air dry the beads for up to 5 minutes while the tube/plate is on the magnetic stand with the lid open. Caution: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack, they are too dry.
Remove the tube/plate from the magnetic stand. Elute the DNA target from the beads by adding 17 Â”L, 0.1X TE .

Mix well by pipetting up and down 10 times, or on a vortex mixer. Incubate for at least 00:02:00 at Room temperature . If necessary, quickly spin the sample to collect the liquid from the sides of the tube or plate wells before placing back on the magnetic stand.
Place the tube/plate on the magnetic stand. After 5 minutes (or when the solution is clear), transfer 15 Â”L to a new PCR tube and store at -20 Â°C .

Assess the library size distribution on an Agilent Bioanalyzer or TapeStation. The sample may need to be diluted before loading. A peak size of ~520 bp is expected (Figure 33). Figure 33: Example of final library pool size distributions on a TapeStation. ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 libraries were generated from 10,000 viral copies. Samples can be stored at â20Â°C. If excess adaptor dimer peak is observed at 150-180 bp, a second 0.7X bead cleanup can be performed. The second 0.7X cleanup will result in slightly larger libraries which will not affect sequencing results.

Set a 100 ÎŒl pipette to 20 ÎŒl and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube.
Place the tube on a thermocycler and perform PCR amplification using the following PCR cycling conditions: %s %s
